L1.area: HTTP URI construction and serialization layer

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where a string reference is unsafely transmuted to a static lifetime and stored in an internal collection. If a user-provided callback panics before the reference is removed from the collection, the transmuted reference persists beyond the original string's lifetime, allowing access to freed memory during or after panic unwinding.

L3.area: URI query parameter formatting and prefix handling logic
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the URI query formatting logic where a string reference is unsafely transmuted to a static lifetime and pushed onto a prefix stack. When a user-provided display callback panics while the prefix is still on the stack, the transmuted reference persists beyond the original string's lifetime, allowing access to freed memory during panic unwinding through Drop implementations or after unwinding via catch_unwind.

ground_truth.reason: The with_prefix method in the URI query formatter transmutes a borrowed string reference to a static lifetime and pushes it onto a stack, but if the user-provided callback panics, the reference is not popped, allowing use-after-free access to the deallocated string.
